title: oxytropis campestris subsp. johannensis
intro: Oxytropis campestris subsp. johannensis, also known as Johann's mountain clover, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Oxytropis campestris subsp. johannensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and roots, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
